title: Admissions
description: Conroe ISD's admissions policy explains who is eligible to enroll in district schools and what the enrollment process looks like. It covers requirements such as residency, age, and documentation needed to register a student. Parents and guardians can use this policy to understand their child's right to attend a Conroe ISD school and what steps to follow when enrolling.

timeline_local.summary: This update to Policy FD (Local) – Admissions adds a new "Students Not Enrolled" section explicitly prohibiting students enrolled in private schools (including homeschools) from concurrent enrollment in the District or participation in curricular or extracurricular activities, in response to SB 401 which would otherwise require districts to allow non-enrolled students to participate in UIL activities.
changes_added: Students Not Enrolled: A student enrolled in a private school, including a homeschool, shall not be eligible for concurrent enrollment in the District nor for participation in curricular or extracurricular activities. [See EEL and FM]
timeline_local.notes: The cover page title reads "Consider and Adopt Revisions to Board Policies FD Local Admissions and FM Local Student Activities Declining to Grant Non-Enrolled Students the Opportunity to Participate in UIL Activities as Required by SB 401," which maps to action = "adopted." The new "Students Not Enrolled" section on page 2 is displayed in blue/underlined text (representing the redline addition); the document labels this as proposed new language. No red strikethrough (removed) language was identified in the FD Local redline pages. The FM Local policy redline was referenced in the cover page but not included in this document set.
